{{commons category|Category:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja}} [[JA:杉桙別命神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11522170}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 杉桙別命神社 |画像 = [[File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, shaden and okusu.jpg|270px]]
{{jalink|杉桙別命神社の大クス|Q60577358|大クス}}（左奥、国の{{jalink|天然記念物|Q23790}}）と社殿（右）
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|45|15.3|N|138|59|20.6|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|河津町|Q1203861}}田中宮ノ脇153 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|45|15.3|N|138|59|20.6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 杉桙別命 |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = 三社間流造銅板葺 |別名 = 川津来宮神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月16日|Q2963}} |神事 = {{jalink|河津の鳥精進酒精進|Q11553983|鳥精進酒精進}}（{{jalink|12月18日|Q2438}} - {{jalink|12月23日|Q2676|23日}}） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=45|lat_sec=15.3|lon_deg=138|lon_min=59|lon_sec=20.6|label=杉桙別命
神社|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} [[File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, otorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|大鳥居}}]] '''杉桙別命神社'''（すぎほこわけのみことじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|河津町|Q1203861}}田中にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。'''川津来宮神社'''（かわづきのみやじんじゃ、「河津」ではない）とも[https://sugihokowakenomikoto.jimdo.com/ 川津来宮神社（杉桙別命神社）公式サイト][http://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/25 川津来宮神社の大楠] - 河津町観光協会。 式内社の「佐々原比咩命神社」に比定される'''{{jalink|#姫宮神社||姫宮神社}}'''を所管する。 === 社名 === 古くは「桙別明神」、「木野（大）明神」、「来宮（大）明神」、「木野神社」、「木之神社」、「鬼崎（きのさき）明神」などと呼ばれており、現在でも「川津来宮神社」と俗称される。「来宮」の古社名や後述する「鳥精進酒精進」を伝承することから、{{jalink|キノミヤ信仰|Q11296898}}を有する神社とされている。 === 祭神 === '''杉桙別命'''（すぎほこわけのみこと）を主祭神とし、{{jalink|五十猛命}}と{{jalink|少彦名命}}を{{jalink|相殿|Q135009883}}に祀る。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』では1座なので、本来は杉桙別命1柱を祀るものであったと思われる『式内社調査報告』。。 === 歴史 === 社伝によると、古代から鎮座していたが{{jalink|和銅|Q1373026}}年間（708 - 15年）に再建されたといい、『延喜式神名帳』で小社に列した。また社伝では、{{jalink|建久|Q1193933}}4年（{{jalink|1193年|Q19741}}）に{{jalink|源頼朝|Q242800}}が再建するとともに{{jalink|社領}}93{{jalink|石 (単位)|Q855048|石}}を奉納し、{{jalink|嘉禄|Q1194421}}2年（{{jalink|1226年|Q5405}}）に{{jalink|藤原頼経|Q471780}}により更に社殿が再築されたという。{{jalink|康永|Q980266}}2年（{{jalink|1343年|Q6072}}）の{{jalink|国内神名帳|Q11420490}}である『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』には「従四位上 ほこわけの明神」と記されている。社伝によるとその後も、{{jalink|永正|Q1193577}}10年（{{jalink|1513年|Q6200}}）に{{jalink|足利義稙|Q708527}}により社殿が再築され、{{jalink|社家|Q11588367}}12家が奉仕したというが、{{jalink|天文 (元号)|Q1192853|天文}}7年（{{jalink|1538年|Q6470}}）に社殿等を悉く焼失し、同13年（{{jalink|1544年|Q6525}}）には洪水によって社領が流されてからは頽廃して小祠での鎮座となったものを、国守代官{{jalink|清水康英|Q11560955}}等が再建したという。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）郷社に列した。{{jalink|戦後|Q1201472}}は{{jalink|神社本庁|Q908077}}に属している。なお、神職は代々{{jalink|河津氏|Q11553993}}が奉仕している。 === 境内 === === 社殿 === 本殿は、{{jalink|嘉祥|Q1373157}}2年（{{jalink|1819年|Q6967}}）の造替にかかる、正面側面ともに一間の{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}。屋根は{{jalink|銅板葺}}で{{jalink|破風#破風の形式|Q4881511|千鳥破風}}、軒{{jalink|破風#破風の形式|Q4881511|唐破風}}を付ける。 File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, honden.jpg|本殿 File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, haiden.jpg|拝殿 File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, torii.jpg|境内鳥居 === 杉桙別命神社の大クス === [[File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, okusu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|[[杉桙別命神社の大クス|大クス]]（国の天然記念物）}}]] {{Main|杉桙別命神社の大クス}} 樹高約24m、幹周14mの{{jalink|クスノキ|Q158722|楠}}。{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}初期（20世紀前葉）時点で樹齢1,000年以上と推定され、昭和11年（{{jalink|1936年|Q18649}}）に国の{{jalink|天然記念物|Q23790}}に指定された。古くより'''来宮様の大クス'''と呼ばれ、{{jalink|神木|Q48743901}}として崇められてきた。かつて河津には7本の大楠があり、明治時代中頃まで'''河津郷七抱七楠'''（ななかかえななくす）と呼ばれていたが、現存しているのはこの1本だけである。また、参道入口付近と、拝殿右前にも楠の大木がある。 なお、{{jalink|伊豆半島|Q822822}}北部にある{{jalink|熱海市|Q653364}}の{{jalink|来宮神社|Q11523682}}にも、{{jalink|阿豆佐和気神社の大クス|Q60403026}}という有名な楠の大木（同じく天然記念物）があり、「来宮神社のクス」と同じ名称で呼ばれることも多いため混同しやすい。 File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja 20100601 (3).jpg|杉桙別命神社の社叢 File:Cinnamomum camphora 20100601 (Kawazu) (B).jpg|参道入口にある楠 === 摂末社 === 道祖神社、宇賀神社、稲荷神社、天神社、水神社、熊野神社、小島神社、歳之神社、火産帰（ほうぶつき）神社、大楠神社、塞神社、山神社、大那行都佐（おおなごつさ）神社 File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|境内社 === 祭事 === 酒留祭（12月17日）・酒開祭（12月24日） - 両祭の間（12月18日 - 23日）は{{jalink|河津の鳥精進酒精進|Q11553983}}と呼ばれ、当地で泥酔した杉桙別命が野火にあい焼死寸前になった時、小鳥の大群が羽に付いた水を運び消火したおかげで一命を取り留めたので、杉桙別命が飲酒とともに鳥肉、卵を食べることを禁じたという。 === 姫宮神社 === {{神社 |名称 = 姫宮神社 |画像 = [[File:Himemiya-jinja (Kawazu), shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
社殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|45|06.1|N|138|59|34.3|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|河津町|Q1203861}}笹原314 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|45|06.1|N|138|59|34.3|E|scale:25000|name=姫宮神社}} |祭神 = 佐々原比咩命 |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = |本殿 = 一間社流造杮葺 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} === 社名 === 古くは「姫宮」、また「姫（妃）宮大明神」と称されていた。 === 祭神 === '''佐々原比咩命'''（ささはらひめのみこと）を主祭神に、明治44年（{{jalink|1911年|Q2076}}）に若宮八幡神社（{{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119|大鷦鷯尊}}（おおさざきのみこと））と蔭山（かげやま）神社（蔭山明神）を合祀した正しくは若宮八幡神社に合祀され、主祭神を佐々原比咩命に変更した。。 === 歴史 === 旧社地（河津町笹原字比咩宮305。現{{jalink|河津町立南小学校}}、同{{jalink|姫宮幼稚園}}の敷地内）付近からは4世紀のものと思われる祭祀遺物が出土しており（{{jalink|姫宮遺跡}}）、古くから{{jalink|産土神|Q1392097}}として祀られていたようである。『延喜式神名帳』で小社に列し、『伊豆国神階帳』では「従四位上 さゝわら姫のみこ」と記されている。社伝によると{{jalink|元亀|Q1145064}}頃（1570 - 73年）までは社領を有したが、{{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}年間（1573 - 92年）に没収され、{{jalink|文禄|Q1373039}}5年（{{jalink|1596年|Q6785}}）に図書という社人が古記録等を持ち去ってから衰退したという。{{jalink|寛文|Q1146228}}4年（{{jalink|1664年|Q7581}}）に河津全村の人々によって再興され、明治6年{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列し、同44年に現社地（厳密には現社地のやや北東）にあった若宮八幡神社に近くの蔭山神社とともに合祀されて、社号を「姫宮神社」、主祭神を'''佐々原比咩命'''に変更し若宮八幡神社は健保年間（1213 - 19年）に再建されたと伝える。、更に昭和38年に杉桙別命神社の本殿脇に遷座されたが、{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}14年（{{jalink|2002年|Q1987}}）に再び返遷した。 === 社殿 === 本殿は正面側面共に一間の{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}で、{{jalink|杮葺|Q199388}}の屋根に千鳥破風、軒に唐破風を付ける。{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}後期の社殿である。 File:Himemiya-jinja (Kawazu), kyushati.jpg|河津町立南小学校（姫宮遺跡） === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === *式内社研究會編『式内社調査報告』第10巻伊豆国・甲斐国、皇學館大學出版部、昭和56年 *谷川健一編『日本の神々－神社と聖地』第10巻東海、白水社、1987年 ISBN 4-560-02220-8 === 交通 === *杉桙別命神社は{{jalink|伊豆急行線|Q864265}}{{jalink|河津駅|Q6379913}}から徒歩15分。途中に姫宮神社の参道入口がある。 === 外部リンク === {{Commonscat|Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja}} {{Commonscat|Himemiya-jinja (Kawazu)|姫宮神社}} *[https://sugihokowakenomikoto.jimdoweb.com/ 川津来宮神社（杉桙別命神社）] - 公式サイト * {{Twitter|kawazukinomiya|川津来宮神社}} * {{Instagram|kawazukinomiyashrine|川津来宮神社（杉桙別命神社）}} {{神道 横}} {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=姫宮神社 (河津町) |1-1=静岡県の神社 |1-2=伊豆国の式内社 |1-3=静岡県の旧村社 |1-4=河津町の建築物 |1-5=河津町の歴史 }} {{DEFAULTSORT:すきほこわけのみことしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:河津町の建築物]] [[Category:河津町の歴史]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{commons category|Category:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja}} [[JA:杉桙別命神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11522170}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine |Image = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|45|15.3|N|138|59|20.6|E}}}} |Location = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}{{jalink|Kawazu Town|Q1203861}}153 Tanaka Miyanowaki |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 degrees minutes, seconds|34|45|15.3|N|138|59|20.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Sugisubetsu no Mikoto |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Founded = unknown |Main shrine = Sanjama flown copper plate roof |Alternative name = Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{jalink|October 16th|Q2963}}|Shinto ritual = {{jalink|Kawazu's Tori Shojin Sake Shojin|Q11553983|Tori Shojin Sake Shojin}} ({{jalink|December 18th|Q2438}} - {{jalink|December 23rd|Q2676|23rd}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=45|lat_sec=15.3|lon_deg=138|lon_min=59|lon_sec=20.6|label=Shinto Torii
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} [[File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, otorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Otorii}}]]'''Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine'' is located in {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} in Tanaka. In {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}. '''Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine'' (not ``Kawazu'') [https://sugihokowakenomikoto.jimdo.com/ Official site of Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine (Sugisawabetsu no Mikoto Shrine)][http://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/25 Large camphor tree of Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine] - Kawazu Town Tourism Association. It is in charge of '''{{jalink|#Himemiya Shrine||Himemiya Shrine}}''', which is compared to ``Sasahara Hime no Mikoto Shrine'', a Shikinai shrine. === Company name === In ancient times, it was called ``Sawabetsu Myojin'', ``Kino (Great) Myojin'', ``Kinomiya (Great) Myojin'', ``Kino Shrine'', ``Kino Shrine'', and ``Kinosaki Myojin'', and even today it is commonly known as ``Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine''. It is said to be a shrine with {{jalink|Kinomiya faith|Q11296898}}, as it has passed down the ancient name of ``Kinomiya'' and ``Tori Shojin Sake Shojin'' (described later). === Enshrined God === The main deity is ``Sugihokowake no Mikoto'', and {{jalink|Isotake no Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Sukuhikona no Mikoto}} are enshrined in {{jalink|Aidono|Q135009883}}. In "{{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932}}", it is listed as 1 dai, so it is thought that it was originally dedicated to Sugisubetsu no Mikoto. . === History ===According to the shrine's legend, it had been enshrined since ancient times, but was rebuilt in the {{jalink|Wado|Q1373026}} period (708 - 15), and was listed as a small shrine in the Engishiki Shinmyocho. In addition, in the shrine biography, in {{jalink|Kenkyu|Q1193933}}4 ({{jalink|1193|Q19741}}), {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800}} rebuilt it and {{jalink|sharyo}}93{{jalink|stone (Unit)|Q855048|stone}} was dedicated, and the shrine building was further rebuilt by {{jalink|Fujiwara Yoritsune|Q471780}} in {{jalink|1226|Q5405}}. In {{jalink|Yasunaga|Q980266}} 2nd year ({{jalink|1343|Q6072}}), {{jalink|Domestic Shinto Name Book|Q11420490}}, ``{{jalink|Izu Province Shinto Name Book|Q17193087}},'' it is written as ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Hokowake no Myojin.'' According to the shrine's legend, the shrine was rebuilt by Yoshitane Ashikaga|Q708527} in {{jalink|Eisho|Q1193577}}10 ({{jalink|1513|Q6200}}), and 12 families served {{jalink|Shake|Q11588367}}. (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} In the 7th year ({{jalink|1538|Q6470}}), the shrine and other buildings were completely destroyed by fire, and in the 13th year of the same year ({{jalink|1544|Q652 In 5}}), after the shrine's territory was washed away by a flood, it fell into disrepair and was enshrined in a small shrine, but it is said that the governor of the country {{jalink|Yasuhide Shimizu|Q11560955}} and others rebuilt it.In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), he was appointed to Gosha. {{jalink|Postwar|Q1201472}} belongs to {{jalink|Shrine Headquarters|Q908077}}. Furthermore, the Shinto priest has been served by the {{jalink|Kawazu clan|Q11553993}} for generations. === Precincts === === Shrine building === The main shrine was rebuilt in {{jalink|Kasho|Q1373157}}2 ({{jalink|1819|Q6967}}), and is {{jalink|Nagarezukuri|Q210684}}. The roof is {{jalink|copper plate roof}} with {{jalink|gable #gable style|Q4881511|staggered gable}} and eaves {{jalink|gable #gable style|Q4881511|kara gable}}. File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, haiden.jpg|House of worship File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii in the precincts === Big Kusu of Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine === [[File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, okusu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|[[Big Kusu of Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja|Big Kusu]] (National Natural Monument)}}]] {{Main|Big Kusu of Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine}} A {{jalink|camphor tree|Q158722|camphor}} with a tree height of about 24m and a trunk circumference of 14m. The tree was estimated to be over 1,000 years old in the early {{jalink|Showa period|Q327567}} period (first half of the 20th century), and was designated as a national {{jalink|natural monument|Q23790}} in 1936|Q18649}}. Since ancient times, it has been called ``Kimiya-sama's great Kusu'' and has been worshiped as {{jalink|sacred tree|Q48743901}}. There were once seven large camphor trees in Kawazu, and until the mid-Meiji period they were called ``Nanaka Kae Nanakusu,'' but this is the only one that remains today. There is also a large camphor tree near the approach entrance and in front of the shrine to the right. In addition, there is also {{jalink|Kinomiya Shrine|Q11523682}} in {{jalink|Atami City|Q653364}} in the northern part of {{jalink|Izu Peninsula|Q822822}}. alink|Azu Sawaki Shrine's Large Kusu|Q60403026}} There is a famous camphor tree (also a natural monument), which is often called the same name as "Kimiya Shrine's Kusu", so it is easy to confuse it. File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja 20100601 (3).jpg|Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja ShrineFile:Cinnamomum camphora 20100601 (Kawazu) (B).jpg|Cinnamomum camphora at the entrance to the approach === Setsusha === Doso Shrine, Uga Shrine, Inari Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Mizu Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Kojima Shrine, Toshino Shrine, Hobutsuki Shrine, Ohgusu Shrine, Sa Shrine, Yama Shrine, Onagotsusa Shrine File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festival === Sakeru Festival (December 17th) / Sake Festival (December 24th) - Between the two festivals (December 18th - 23rd) is called {{jalink|Kawazu's Tori Shojin Sake Shojin|Q11553983}}, and it is said that when Sugisabetsu no Mikoto, who was drunk in the area, was about to burn to death in a wild fire, he was saved thanks to a large flock of small birds carrying water from their wings and extinguishing the fire, so Sugisabetsu no Mikoto forbade him to drink alcohol and eat poultry and eggs. === Himemiya Shrine === {{Shrine |Name = Himemiya Shrine |Image = [[File:Himemiya-jinja (Kawazu), shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
Shrine building
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|45|06.1|N|138|59|34.3|E}}}} |Location = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}{{jalink|Kawazu Town|Q1203861}}Sasahara 314 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|45|06.1|N|138|59|34.3|E|scale:25000|name=Himemiya Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = Sasahara Hime no Mikoto |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} |Soken = |Main shrine = Ikkensha-ryu-zukuri thatched roof |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = |Shinto ritual = }} === Company name === In ancient times, it was called ``Himemiya'' or ``Himemiya Daimyojin.'' === Enshrined God === Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine ({{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q3 13119|Osazaki no Mikoto) and Kageyama Shrine (Kageyama Myojin) were enshrined togetherProperly, it was enshrined at Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, and the main deity was changed to Sasahara Hime no Mikoto. . === History === Ritual artifacts thought to date back to the 4th century have been unearthed near the former shrine grounds (305 Sasahara Himamiya, Kawazu-machi, currently on the grounds of {{jalink|Kawazu Town Minami Elementary School}} and {{jalink|Himemiya Kindergarten}}) ({{jalink|Himemiya Ruins}}), and it appears that it has been worshiped as a {{jalink|Tsubojin|Q1392097}} since ancient times. It is listed as a small shrine in the ``Engishiki Shinmyocho'', and is described as ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper Sawarahime no Miko'' in the ``Izu Province Shinto Namecho''. According to the shrine's biography, the shrine had territory until around {{jalink|Motogame|Q1145064}} (1570 - 73), but for the {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} years (1573 - It is said that it was confiscated in 1992), and in {{jalink|Bunroku|Q1373039}}5 ({{jalink|1596|Q6785}}), a company official called Tosho took away the old records and declined. It was rebuilt by the people of the entire Kawazu village in {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 4th year ({{jalink|1664|Q7581}}), and was listed in {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} in 1896, In 1213, Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, which was located at the current site (slightly northeast of the current site), was enshrined together with the nearby Kageyama Shrine, and the company name was changed to ``Himemiya Shrine'' and the main deity was changed to ``Sasahara Hime no Mikoto''. - It was rebuilt in 2019). , In 1962, it was moved to the side of the main shrine of Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine, but it was moved again in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}14 ({{jalink|2002|Q1987}}). === Shrine building === The main shrine is one-span {{jalink|nagaru-zukuri|Q210684}} on both the front and sides, with a {{jalink|杮thatched|Q199388}} roof with a staggered gable and a kara gable on the eaves. It is a late {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} shrine building. File:Himemiya-jinja (Kawazu), kyushati.jpg|Kawazu Town Minami Elementary School (Himemiya Ruins) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === References === *Shikinaisha Research Report, edited by Shikinaisha Research Association, Volume 10, Izu Province, Kai Province, Kogakkan University Publishing Department, 1981. *Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, edited by Kenichi Tanigawa, Volume 10, Tokai, Hakusuisha, 1987 ISBN 4-560-02220-8 === Transportation === *Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine is a 15-minute walk from {{jalink|Izukyu Line|Q864265}}{{jalink|Kawazu Station|Q6379913}}. Along the way is the entrance to Himemiya Shrine. === External link === {{Commonscat|Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja}} {{Commonscat|Himemiya-jinja (Kawazu)|Himemiya Shrine}} *[https://sugihokowakenomikoto.jimdoweb.com/ Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine (Sugisawa Betsu no Mikoto Shrine)] - Official website * {{Twitter|kawazukinomiya|Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine}} * {{Instagram|kawazukinomiyashrine|Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine (Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine)}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Redirect belongs to category |redirect1=Himemiya Shrine (Kawazu Town) |1-1=Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture |1-2=Shikinaisha in Izunokuni |1-3=Old village shrine in Shizuoka prefecture |1-4=Buildings in Kawazu Town |1-5=History of Kawazu Town }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sukihokowa Mikoto Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:河津町の建築物]] [[Category:河津町の歴史]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{commons category|Category:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja}} [[JA:杉桙別命神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11522170}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine |Image = [[File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, shaden and okusu.jpg|270px]]
{{jalink|Big Kusu of Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, shaden and okusu.jpg|Q60577358|Big Kusu}} (back left, national {{jalink|natural monument|Q23790}}) and shrine building (right)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|45|15.3|N|138|59|20.6|E}}}} |Location = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}{{jalink|Kawazu Town|Q1203861}}153 Tanaka Miyanowaki |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 degrees minutes, seconds|34|45|15.3|N|138|59|20.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Sugisubetsu no Mikoto |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Founded = unknown |Main shrine = Sanjama flown copper plate roof |Alternative name = Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{jalink|October 16th|Q2963}}|Shinto ritual = {{jalink|Kawazu's Tori Shojin Sake Shojin|Q11553983|Tori Shojin Sake Shojin}} ({{jalink|December 18th|Q2438}} - {{jalink|December 23rd|Q2676|23rd}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=45|lat_sec=15.3|lon_deg=138|lon_min=59|lon_sec=20.6|label=Shinto Torii
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} [[File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, otorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Otorii}}]]'''Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine'' is located in {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} in Tanaka. In {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}. '''Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine'' (not ``Kawazu'') [https://sugihokowakenomikoto.jimdo.com/ Official site of Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine (Sugisawabetsu no Mikoto Shrine)][http://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/25 Large camphor tree of Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine] - Kawazu Town Tourism Association. It is in charge of '''{{jalink|#Himemiya Shrine||Himemiya Shrine}}''', which is compared to ``Sasahara Hime no Mikoto Shrine'', a Shikinai shrine. === Company name === In ancient times, it was called ``Sawabetsu Myojin'', ``Kino (Great) Myojin'', ``Kinomiya (Great) Myojin'', ``Kino Shrine'', ``Kino Shrine'', and ``Kinosaki Myojin'', and even today it is commonly known as ``Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine''. It is said to be a shrine with {{jalink|Kinomiya faith|Q11296898}}, as it has passed down the ancient name of ``Kinomiya'' and ``Tori Shojin Sake Shojin'' (described later). === Enshrined God === The main deity is ``Sugihokowake no Mikoto'', and {{jalink|Isotake no Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Sukuhikona no Mikoto}} are enshrined in {{jalink|Aidono|Q135009883}}. In "{{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932}}", it is listed as 1 dai, so it is thought that it was originally dedicated to Sugisubetsu no Mikoto. . === History ===According to the shrine's legend, it had been enshrined since ancient times, but was rebuilt in the {{jalink|Wado|Q1373026}} period (708 - 15), and was listed as a small shrine in the Engishiki Shinmyocho. In addition, in the shrine biography, in {{jalink|Kenkyu|Q1193933}}4 ({{jalink|1193|Q19741}}), {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800}} rebuilt it and {{jalink|sharyo}}93{{jalink|stone (Unit)|Q855048|stone}} was dedicated, and the shrine building was further rebuilt by {{jalink|Fujiwara Yoritsune|Q471780}} in {{jalink|1226|Q5405}}. In {{jalink|Yasunaga|Q980266}} 2nd year ({{jalink|1343|Q6072}}), {{jalink|Domestic Shinto Name Book|Q11420490}}, ``{{jalink|Izu Province Shinto Name Book|Q17193087}},'' it is written as ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Hokowake no Myojin.'' According to the shrine's legend, the shrine was rebuilt by Yoshitane Ashikaga|Q708527} in {{jalink|Eisho|Q1193577}}10 ({{jalink|1513|Q6200}}), and 12 families served {{jalink|Shake|Q11588367}}. (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} In the 7th year ({{jalink|1538|Q6470}}), the shrine and other buildings were completely destroyed by fire, and in the 13th year of the same year ({{jalink|1544|Q652 In 5}}), after the shrine's territory was washed away by a flood, it fell into disrepair and was enshrined in a small shrine, but it is said that the governor of the country {{jalink|Yasuhide Shimizu|Q11560955}} and others rebuilt it.In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), he was appointed to Gosha. {{jalink|Postwar|Q1201472}} belongs to {{jalink|Shrine Headquarters|Q908077}}. Furthermore, the Shinto priest has been served by the {{jalink|Kawazu clan|Q11553993}} for generations. === Precincts === === Shrine building === The main shrine was rebuilt in {{jalink|Kasho|Q1373157}}2 ({{jalink|1819|Q6967}}), and is {{jalink|Nagarezukuri|Q210684}}. The roof is {{jalink|copper plate roof}} with {{jalink|gable #gable style|Q4881511|staggered gable}} and eaves {{jalink|gable #gable style|Q4881511|kara gable}}. File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, haiden.jpg|House of worship File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii in the precincts === Big Kusu of Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine === [[File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, okusu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|[[Big Kusu of Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja|Big Kusu]] (National Natural Monument)}}]] {{Main|Big Kusu of Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine}} A {{jalink|camphor tree|Q158722|camphor}} with a tree height of about 24m and a trunk circumference of 14m. The tree was estimated to be over 1,000 years old in the early {{jalink|Showa period|Q327567}} period (first half of the 20th century), and was designated as a national {{jalink|natural monument|Q23790}} in 1936|Q18649}}. Since ancient times, it has been called ``Kimiya-sama's great Kusu'' and has been worshiped as {{jalink|sacred tree|Q48743901}}. There were once seven large camphor trees in Kawazu, and until the mid-Meiji period they were called ``Nanaka Kae Nanakusu,'' but this is the only one that remains today. There is also a large camphor tree near the approach entrance and in front of the shrine to the right. In addition, there is also {{jalink|Kinomiya Shrine|Q11523682}} in {{jalink|Atami City|Q653364}} in the northern part of {{jalink|Izu Peninsula|Q822822}}. alink|Azu Sawaki Shrine's Large Kusu|Q60403026}} There is a famous camphor tree (also a natural monument), which is often called the same name as "Kimiya Shrine's Kusu", so it is easy to confuse it. File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja 20100601 (3).jpg|Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja ShrineFile:Cinnamomum camphora 20100601 (Kawazu) (B).jpg|Cinnamomum camphora at the entrance to the approach === Setsusha === Doso Shrine, Uga Shrine, Inari Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Mizu Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Kojima Shrine, Toshino Shrine, Hobutsuki Shrine, Ohgusu Shrine, Sa Shrine, Yama Shrine, Onagotsusa Shrine File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festival === Sakeru Festival (December 17th) / Sake Festival (December 24th) - Between the two festivals (December 18th - 23rd) is called {{jalink|Kawazu's Tori Shojin Sake Shojin|Q11553983}}, and it is said that when Sugisabetsu no Mikoto, who was drunk in the area, was about to burn to death in a wild fire, he was saved thanks to a large flock of small birds carrying water from their wings and extinguishing the fire, so Sugisabetsu no Mikoto forbade him to drink alcohol and eat poultry and eggs. === Himemiya Shrine === {{Shrine |Name = Himemiya Shrine |Image = [[File:Himemiya-jinja (Kawazu), shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
Shrine building
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|45|06.1|N|138|59|34.3|E}}}} |Location = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}{{jalink|Kawazu Town|Q1203861}}Sasahara 314 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|45|06.1|N|138|59|34.3|E|scale:25000|name=Himemiya Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = Sasahara Hime no Mikoto |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} |Soken = |Main shrine = Ikkensha-ryu-zukuri thatched roof |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = |Shinto ritual = }} === Company name === In ancient times, it was called ``Himemiya'' or ``Himemiya Daimyojin.'' === Enshrined God === Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine ({{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q3 13119|Osazaki no Mikoto) and Kageyama Shrine (Kageyama Myojin) were enshrined togetherProperly, it was enshrined at Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, and the main deity was changed to Sasahara Hime no Mikoto. . === History === Ritual artifacts thought to date back to the 4th century have been unearthed near the former shrine grounds (305 Sasahara Himamiya, Kawazu-machi, currently on the grounds of {{jalink|Kawazu Town Minami Elementary School}} and {{jalink|Himemiya Kindergarten}}) ({{jalink|Himemiya Ruins}}), and it appears that it has been worshiped as a {{jalink|Tsubojin|Q1392097}} since ancient times. It is listed as a small shrine in the ``Engishiki Shinmyocho'', and is described as ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper Sawarahime no Miko'' in the ``Izu Province Shinto Namecho''. According to the shrine's biography, the shrine had territory until around {{jalink|Motogame|Q1145064}} (1570 - 73), but for the {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} years (1573 - It is said that it was confiscated in 1992), and in {{jalink|Bunroku|Q1373039}}5 ({{jalink|1596|Q6785}}), a company official called Tosho took away the old records and declined. It was rebuilt by the people of the entire Kawazu village in {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 4th year ({{jalink|1664|Q7581}}), and was listed in {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} in 1896, In 1213, Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, which was located at the current site (slightly northeast of the current site), was enshrined together with the nearby Kageyama Shrine, and the company name was changed to ``Himemiya Shrine'' and the main deity was changed to ``Sasahara Hime no Mikoto''. - It was rebuilt in 2019). , In 1962, it was moved to the side of the main shrine of Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine, but it was moved again in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}14 ({{jalink|2002|Q1987}}). === Shrine building === The main shrine is one-span {{jalink|nagaru-zukuri|Q210684}} on both the front and sides, with a {{jalink|杮thatched|Q199388}} roof with a staggered gable and a kara gable on the eaves. It is a late {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} shrine building. File:Himemiya-jinja (Kawazu), kyushati.jpg|Kawazu Town Minami Elementary School (Himemiya Ruins) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === References === *Shikinaisha Research Report, edited by Shikinaisha Research Association, Volume 10, Izu Province, Kai Province, Kogakkan University Publishing Department, 1981. *Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, edited by Kenichi Tanigawa, Volume 10, Tokai, Hakusuisha, 1987 ISBN 4-560-02220-8 === Transportation === *Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine is a 15-minute walk from {{jalink|Izukyu Line|Q864265}}{{jalink|Kawazu Station|Q6379913}}. Along the way is the entrance to Himemiya Shrine. === External link === {{Commonscat|Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja}} {{Commonscat|Himemiya-jinja (Kawazu)|Himemiya Shrine}} *[https://sugihokowakenomikoto.jimdoweb.com/ Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine (Sugisawa Betsu no Mikoto Shrine)] - Official website * {{Twitter|kawazukinomiya|Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine}} * {{Instagram|kawazukinomiyashrine|Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine (Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine)}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Redirect belongs to category |redirect1=Himemiya Shrine (Kawazu Town) |1-1=Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture |1-2=Shikinaisha in Izunokuni |1-3=Old village shrine in Shizuoka prefecture |1-4=Buildings in Kawazu Town |1-5=History of Kawazu Town }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sukihokowa Mikoto Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:河津町の建築物]] [[Category:河津町の歴史]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{commons category|Category:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja}} [[JA:杉桙別命神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11522170}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine |Image = [[File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, shaden and okusu.jpg|270px]]
{{jalink|Big Kusu of Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, shaden and okusu.jpg|Q60577358|Big Kusu}} (back left, national {{jalink|natural monument|Q23790}}) and shrine building (right)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|45|15.3|N|138|59|20.6|E}}}} |Location = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}{{jalink|Kawazu Town|Q1203861}}153 Tanaka Miyanowaki |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 degrees minutes, seconds|34|45|15.3|N|138|59|20.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Sugisubetsu no Mikoto |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Founded = unknown |Main shrine = Sanjama flown copper plate roof |Alternative name = Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{jalink|October 16th|Q2963}}|Shinto ritual = {{jalink|Kawazu's Tori Shojin Sake Shojin|Q11553983|Tori Shojin Sake Shojin}} ({{jalink|December 18th|Q2438}} - {{jalink|December 23rd|Q2676|23rd}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=45|lat_sec=15.3|lon_deg=138|lon_min=59|lon_sec=20.6|label=Shinto Torii
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} [[File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, otorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Otorii}}]]'''Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine'' is located in {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} in Tanaka. In {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}. '''Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine'' (not ``Kawazu'') [https://sugihokowakenomikoto.jimdo.com/ Official site of Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine (Sugisawabetsu no Mikoto Shrine)][http://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/25 Large camphor tree of Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine] - Kawazu Town Tourism Association. It is in charge of '''{{jalink|#Himemiya Shrine||Himemiya Shrine}}''', which is compared to ``Sasahara Hime no Mikoto Shrine'', a Shikinai shrine. === Company name === In ancient times, it was called ``Sawabetsu Myojin'', ``Kino (Great) Myojin'', ``Kinomiya (Great) Myojin'', ``Kino Shrine'', ``Kino Shrine'', and ``Kinosaki Myojin'', and even today it is commonly known as ``Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine''. It is said to be a shrine with {{jalink|Kinomiya faith|Q11296898}}, as it has passed down the ancient name of ``Kinomiya'' and ``Tori Shojin Sake Shojin'' (described later). === Enshrined God === The main deity is ``Sugihokowake no Mikoto'', and {{jalink|Isotake no Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Sukuhikona no Mikoto}} are enshrined in {{jalink|Aidono|Q135009883}}. In "{{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932}}", it is listed as 1 dai, so it is thought that it was originally dedicated to Sugisubetsu no Mikoto. . === History ===According to the shrine's legend, it had been enshrined since ancient times, but was rebuilt in the {{jalink|Wado|Q1373026}} period (708 - 15), and was listed as a small shrine in the Engishiki Shinmyocho. In addition, in the shrine biography, in {{jalink|Kenkyu|Q1193933}}4 ({{jalink|1193|Q19741}}), {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800}} rebuilt it and {{jalink|sharyo}}93{{jalink|stone (Unit)|Q855048|stone}} was dedicated, and the shrine building was further rebuilt by {{jalink|Fujiwara Yoritsune|Q471780}} in {{jalink|1226|Q5405}}. In {{jalink|Yasunaga|Q980266}} 2nd year ({{jalink|1343|Q6072}}), {{jalink|Domestic Shinto Name Book|Q11420490}}, ``{{jalink|Izu Province Shinto Name Book|Q17193087}},'' it is written as ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Hokowake no Myojin.'' According to the shrine's legend, the shrine was rebuilt by Yoshitane Ashikaga|Q708527} in {{jalink|Eisho|Q1193577}}10 ({{jalink|1513|Q6200}}), and 12 families served {{jalink|Shake|Q11588367}}. (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} In the 7th year ({{jalink|1538|Q6470}}), the shrine and other buildings were completely destroyed by fire, and in the 13th year of the same year ({{jalink|1544|Q652 In 5}}), after the shrine's territory was washed away by a flood, it fell into disrepair and was enshrined in a small shrine, but it is said that the governor of the country {{jalink|Yasuhide Shimizu|Q11560955}} and others rebuilt it.In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), he was appointed to Gosha. {{jalink|Postwar|Q1201472}} belongs to {{jalink|Shrine Headquarters|Q908077}}. Furthermore, the Shinto priest has been served by the {{jalink|Kawazu clan|Q11553993}} for generations. === Precincts === === Shrine building === The main shrine was rebuilt in {{jalink|Kasho|Q1373157}}2 ({{jalink|1819|Q6967}}), and is {{jalink|Nagarezukuri|Q210684}}. The roof is {{jalink|copper plate roof}} with {{jalink|gable #gable style|Q4881511|staggered gable}} and eaves {{jalink|gable #gable style|Q4881511|kara gable}}. File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, haiden.jpg|House of worship File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii in the precincts === Big Kusu of Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine === [[File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, okusu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|[[Big Kusu of Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja|Big Kusu]] (National Natural Monument)}}]] {{Main|Big Kusu of Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine}} A {{jalink|camphor tree|Q158722|camphor}} with a tree height of about 24m and a trunk circumference of 14m. The tree was estimated to be over 1,000 years old in the early {{jalink|Showa period|Q327567}} period (first half of the 20th century), and was designated as a national {{jalink|natural monument|Q23790}} in 1936|Q18649}}. Since ancient times, it has been called ``Kimiya-sama's great Kusu'' and has been worshiped as {{jalink|sacred tree|Q48743901}}. There were once seven large camphor trees in Kawazu, and until the mid-Meiji period they were called ``Nanaka Kae Nanakusu,'' but this is the only one that remains today. There is also a large camphor tree near the approach entrance and in front of the shrine to the right. In addition, there is also {{jalink|Kinomiya Shrine|Q11523682}} in {{jalink|Atami City|Q653364}} in the northern part of {{jalink|Izu Peninsula|Q822822}}. alink|Azu Sawaki Shrine's Large Kusu|Q60403026}} There is a famous camphor tree (also a natural monument), which is often called the same name as "Kimiya Shrine's Kusu", so it is easy to confuse it. File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja 20100601 (3).jpg|Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja ShrineFile:Cinnamomum camphora 20100601 (Kawazu) (B).jpg|Cinnamomum camphora at the entrance to the approach === Setsusha === Doso Shrine, Uga Shrine, Inari Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Mizu Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Kojima Shrine, Toshino Shrine, Hobutsuki Shrine, Ohgusu Shrine, Sa Shrine, Yama Shrine, Onagotsusa Shrine File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festival === Sakeru Festival (December 17th) / Sake Festival (December 24th) - Between the two festivals (December 18th - 23rd) is called {{jalink|Kawazu's Tori Shojin Sake Shojin|Q11553983}}, and it is said that when Sugisabetsu no Mikoto, who was drunk in the area, was about to burn to death in a wild fire, he was saved thanks to a large flock of small birds carrying water from their wings and extinguishing the fire, so Sugisabetsu no Mikoto forbade him to drink alcohol and eat poultry and eggs. === Himemiya Shrine === {{Shrine |Name = Himemiya Shrine |Image = [[File:Himemiya-jinja (Kawazu), shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
Shrine building
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|45|06.1|N|138|59|34.3|E}}}} |Location = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}{{jalink|Kawazu Town|Q1203861}}Sasahara 314 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|45|06.1|N|138|59|34.3|E|scale:25000|name=Himemiya Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = Sasahara Hime no Mikoto |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} |Soken = |Main shrine = Ikkensha-ryu-zukuri thatched roof |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = |Shinto ritual = }} === Company name === In ancient times, it was called ``Himemiya'' or ``Himemiya Daimyojin.'' === Enshrined God === Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine ({{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q3 13119|Osazaki no Mikoto) and Kageyama Shrine (Kageyama Myojin) were enshrined togetherProperly, it was enshrined at Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, and the main deity was changed to Sasahara Hime no Mikoto. . === History === Ritual artifacts thought to date back to the 4th century have been unearthed near the former shrine grounds (305 Sasahara Himamiya, Kawazu-machi, currently on the grounds of {{jalink|Kawazu Town Minami Elementary School}} and {{jalink|Himemiya Kindergarten}}) ({{jalink|Himemiya Ruins}}), and it appears that it has been worshiped as a {{jalink|Tsubojin|Q1392097}} since ancient times. It is listed as a small shrine in the ``Engishiki Shinmyocho'', and is described as ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper Sawarahime no Miko'' in the ``Izu Province Shinto Namecho''. According to the shrine's biography, the shrine had territory until around {{jalink|Motogame|Q1145064}} (1570 - 73), but for the {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} years (1573 - It is said that it was confiscated in 1992), and in {{jalink|Bunroku|Q1373039}}5 ({{jalink|1596|Q6785}}), a company official called Tosho took away the old records and declined. It was rebuilt by the people of the entire Kawazu village in {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 4th year ({{jalink|1664|Q7581}}), and was listed in {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} in 1896, In 1213, Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, which was located at the current site (slightly northeast of the current site), was enshrined together with the nearby Kageyama Shrine, and the company name was changed to ``Himemiya Shrine'' and the main deity was changed to ``Sasahara Hime no Mikoto''. - It was rebuilt in 2019). , In 1962, it was moved to the side of the main shrine of Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine, but it was moved again in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}14 ({{jalink|2002|Q1987}}). === Shrine building === The main shrine is one-span {{jalink|nagaru-zukuri|Q210684}} on both the front and sides, with a {{jalink|杮thatched|Q199388}} roof with a staggered gable and a kara gable on the eaves. It is a late {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} shrine building. File:Himemiya-jinja (Kawazu), kyushati.jpg|Kawazu Town Minami Elementary School (Himemiya Ruins) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === References === *Shikinaisha Research Report, edited by Shikinaisha Research Association, Volume 10, Izu Province, Kai Province, Kogakkan University Publishing Department, 1981. *Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, edited by Kenichi Tanigawa, Volume 10, Tokai, Hakusuisha, 1987 ISBN 4-560-02220-8 === Transportation === *Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine is a 15-minute walk from {{jalink|Izukyu Line|Q864265}}{{jalink|Kawazu Station|Q6379913}}. Along the way is the entrance to Himemiya Shrine. === External link === {{Commonscat|Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja}} {{Commonscat|Himemiya-jinja (Kawazu)|Himemiya Shrine}} *[https://sugihokowakenomikoto.jimdoweb.com/ Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine (Sugisawa Betsu no Mikoto Shrine)] - Official website * {{Twitter|kawazukinomiya|Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine}} * {{Instagram|kawazukinomiyashrine|Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine (Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine)}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Redirect belongs to category |redirect1=Himemiya Shrine (Kawazu Town) |1-1=Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture |1-2=Shikinaisha in Izunokuni |1-3=Old village shrine in Shizuoka prefecture |1-4=Buildings in Kawazu Town |1-5=History of Kawazu Town }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sukihokowa Mikoto Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:河津町の建築物]] [[Category:河津町の歴史]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{commons category|Category:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja}} [[JA:杉桙別命神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11522170}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine |Image = [[File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, shaden and okusu.jpg|270px]]
{{jalink|Big Kusu of Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, shaden and okusu.jpg|Q60577358|Big Kusu}} (back left, national {{jalink|natural monument|Q23790}}) and shrine building (right)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|45|15.3|N|138|59|20.6|E}}}} |Location = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}{{jalink|Kawazu Town|Q1203861}}153 Tanaka Miyanowaki |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 degrees minutes, seconds|34|45|15.3|N|138|59|20.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Sugisubetsu no Mikoto |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Founded = unknown |Main shrine = Sanjama flown copper plate roof |Alternative name = Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{jalink|October 16th|Q2963}}|Shinto ritual = {{jalink|Kawazu's Tori Shojin Sake Shojin|Q11553983|Tori Shojin Sake Shojin}} ({{jalink|December 18th|Q2438}} - {{jalink|December 23rd|Q2676|23rd}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=45|lat_sec=15.3|lon_deg=138|lon_min=59|lon_sec=20.6|label=Shinto Torii
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} [[File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, otorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Otorii}}]]'''Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine'' is located in {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} in Tanaka. In {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}. '''Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine'' (not ``Kawazu'') [https://sugihokowakenomikoto.jimdo.com/ Official site of Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine (Sugisawabetsu no Mikoto Shrine)][http://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/25 Large camphor tree of Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine] - Kawazu Town Tourism Association. It is in charge of '''{{jalink|#Himemiya Shrine||Himemiya Shrine}}''', which is compared to ``Sasahara Hime no Mikoto Shrine'', a Shikinai shrine. === Company name === In ancient times, it was called ``Sawabetsu Myojin'', ``Kino (Great) Myojin'', ``Kinomiya (Great) Myojin'', ``Kino Shrine'', ``Kino Shrine'', and ``Kinosaki Myojin'', and even today it is commonly known as ``Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine''. It is said to be a shrine with {{jalink|Kinomiya faith|Q11296898}}, as it has passed down the ancient name of ``Kinomiya'' and ``Tori Shojin Sake Shojin'' (described later). === Enshrined God === The main deity is ``Sugihokowake no Mikoto'', and {{jalink|Isotake no Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Sukuhikona no Mikoto}} are enshrined in {{jalink|Aidono|Q135009883}}. In "{{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932}}", it is listed as 1 dai, so it is thought that it was originally dedicated to Sugisubetsu no Mikoto. . === History ===According to the shrine's legend, it had been enshrined since ancient times, but was rebuilt in the {{jalink|Wado|Q1373026}} period (708 - 15), and was listed as a small shrine in the Engishiki Shinmyocho. In addition, in the shrine biography, in {{jalink|Kenkyu|Q1193933}}4 ({{jalink|1193|Q19741}}), {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800}} rebuilt it and {{jalink|sharyo}}93{{jalink|stone (Unit)|Q855048|stone}} was dedicated, and the shrine building was further rebuilt by {{jalink|Fujiwara Yoritsune|Q471780}} in {{jalink|1226|Q5405}}. In {{jalink|Yasunaga|Q980266}} 2nd year ({{jalink|1343|Q6072}}), {{jalink|Domestic Shinto Name Book|Q11420490}}, ``{{jalink|Izu Province Shinto Name Book|Q17193087}},'' it is written as ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Hokowake no Myojin.'' According to the shrine's legend, the shrine was rebuilt by Yoshitane Ashikaga|Q708527} in {{jalink|Eisho|Q1193577}}10 ({{jalink|1513|Q6200}}), and 12 families served {{jalink|Shake|Q11588367}}. (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} In the 7th year ({{jalink|1538|Q6470}}), the shrine and other buildings were completely destroyed by fire, and in the 13th year of the same year ({{jalink|1544|Q652 In 5}}), after the shrine's territory was washed away by a flood, it fell into disrepair and was enshrined in a small shrine, but it is said that the governor of the country {{jalink|Yasuhide Shimizu|Q11560955}} and others rebuilt it.In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), he was appointed to Gosha. {{jalink|Postwar|Q1201472}} belongs to {{jalink|Shrine Headquarters|Q908077}}. Furthermore, the Shinto priest has been served by the {{jalink|Kawazu clan|Q11553993}} for generations. === Precincts === === Shrine building === The main shrine was rebuilt in {{jalink|Kasho|Q1373157}}2 ({{jalink|1819|Q6967}}), and is {{jalink|Nagarezukuri|Q210684}}. The roof is {{jalink|copper plate roof}} with {{jalink|gable #gable style|Q4881511|staggered gable}} and eaves {{jalink|gable #gable style|Q4881511|kara gable}}. File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, haiden.jpg|House of worship File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii in the precincts === Big Kusu of Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine === [[File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, okusu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|[[Big Kusu of Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja|Big Kusu]] (National Natural Monument)}}]] {{Main|Big Kusu of Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine}} A {{jalink|camphor tree|Q158722|camphor}} with a tree height of about 24m and a trunk circumference of 14m. The tree was estimated to be over 1,000 years old in the early {{jalink|Showa period|Q327567}} period (first half of the 20th century), and was designated as a national {{jalink|natural monument|Q23790}} in 1936|Q18649}}. Since ancient times, it has been called ``Kimiya-sama's great Kusu'' and has been worshiped as {{jalink|sacred tree|Q48743901}}. There were once seven large camphor trees in Kawazu, and until the mid-Meiji period they were called ``Nanaka Kae Nanakusu,'' but this is the only one that remains today. There is also a large camphor tree near the approach entrance and in front of the shrine to the right. In addition, there is also {{jalink|Kinomiya Shrine|Q11523682}} in {{jalink|Atami City|Q653364}} in the northern part of {{jalink|Izu Peninsula|Q822822}}. alink|Azu Sawaki Shrine's Large Kusu|Q60403026}} There is a famous camphor tree (also a natural monument), which is often called the same name as "Kimiya Shrine's Kusu", so it is easy to confuse it. File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja 20100601 (3).jpg|Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja ShrineFile:Cinnamomum camphora 20100601 (Kawazu) (B).jpg|Cinnamomum camphora at the entrance to the approach === Setsusha === Doso Shrine, Uga Shrine, Inari Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Mizu Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Kojima Shrine, Toshino Shrine, Hobutsuki Shrine, Ohgusu Shrine, Sa Shrine, Yama Shrine, Onagotsusa Shrine File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festival === Sakeru Festival (December 17th) / Sake Festival (December 24th) - Between the two festivals (December 18th - 23rd) is called {{jalink|Kawazu's Tori Shojin Sake Shojin|Q11553983}}, and it is said that when Sugisabetsu no Mikoto, who was drunk in the area, was about to burn to death in a wild fire, he was saved thanks to a large flock of small birds carrying water from their wings and extinguishing the fire, so Sugisabetsu no Mikoto forbade him to drink alcohol and eat poultry and eggs. === Himemiya Shrine === {{Shrine |Name = Himemiya Shrine |Image = [[File:Himemiya-jinja (Kawazu), shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
Shrine building
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|45|06.1|N|138|59|34.3|E}}}} |Location = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}{{jalink|Kawazu Town|Q1203861}}Sasahara 314 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|45|06.1|N|138|59|34.3|E|scale:25000|name=Himemiya Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = Sasahara Hime no Mikoto |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} |Soken = |Main shrine = Ikkensha-ryu-zukuri thatched roof |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = |Shinto ritual = }} === Company name === In ancient times, it was called ``Himemiya'' or ``Himemiya Daimyojin.'' === Enshrined God === Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine ({{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q3 13119|Osazaki no Mikoto) and Kageyama Shrine (Kageyama Myojin) were enshrined togetherProperly, it was enshrined at Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, and the main deity was changed to Sasahara Hime no Mikoto. . === History === Ritual artifacts thought to date back to the 4th century have been unearthed near the former shrine grounds (305 Sasahara Himamiya, Kawazu-machi, currently on the grounds of {{jalink|Kawazu Town Minami Elementary School}} and {{jalink|Himemiya Kindergarten}}) ({{jalink|Himemiya Ruins}}), and it appears that it has been worshiped as a {{jalink|Tsubojin|Q1392097}} since ancient times. It is listed as a small shrine in the ``Engishiki Shinmyocho'', and is described as ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper Sawarahime no Miko'' in the ``Izu Province Shinto Namecho''. According to the shrine's biography, the shrine had territory until around {{jalink|Motogame|Q1145064}} (1570 - 73), but for the {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} years (1573 - It is said that it was confiscated in 1992), and in {{jalink|Bunroku|Q1373039}}5 ({{jalink|1596|Q6785}}), a company official called Tosho took away the old records and declined. It was rebuilt by the people of the entire Kawazu village in {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 4th year ({{jalink|1664|Q7581}}), and was listed in {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} in 1896, In 1213, Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, which was located at the current site (slightly northeast of the current site), was enshrined together with the nearby Kageyama Shrine, and the company name was changed to ``Himemiya Shrine'' and the main deity was changed to ``Sasahara Hime no Mikoto''. - It was rebuilt in 2019). , In 1962, it was moved to the side of the main shrine of Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine, but it was moved again in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}14 ({{jalink|2002|Q1987}}). === Shrine building === The main shrine is one-span {{jalink|nagaru-zukuri|Q210684}} on both the front and sides, with a {{jalink|杮thatched|Q199388}} roof with a staggered gable and a kara gable on the eaves. It is a late {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} shrine building. File:Himemiya-jinja (Kawazu), kyushati.jpg|Kawazu Town Minami Elementary School (Himemiya Ruins) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === References === *Shikinaisha Research Report, edited by Shikinaisha Research Association, Volume 10, Izu Province, Kai Province, Kogakkan University Publishing Department, 1981. *Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, edited by Kenichi Tanigawa, Volume 10, Tokai, Hakusuisha, 1987 ISBN 4-560-02220-8 === Transportation === *Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine is a 15-minute walk from {{jalink|Izukyu Line|Q864265}}{{jalink|Kawazu Station|Q6379913}}. Along the way is the entrance to Himemiya Shrine. === External link === {{Commonscat|Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja}} {{Commonscat|Himemiya-jinja (Kawazu)|Himemiya Shrine}} *[https://sugihokowakenomikoto.jimdoweb.com/ Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine (Sugisawa Betsu no Mikoto Shrine)] - Official website * {{Twitter|kawazukinomiya|Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine}} * {{Instagram|kawazukinomiyashrine|Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine (Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine)}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Redirect belongs to category |redirect1=Himemiya Shrine (Kawazu Town) |1-1=Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture |1-2=Shikinaisha in Izunokuni |1-3=Old village shrine in Shizuoka prefecture |1-4=Buildings in Kawazu Town |1-5=History of Kawazu Town }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sukihokowa Mikoto Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:河津町の建築物]] [[Category:河津町の歴史]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Founded = unknown |Main shrine = Sanjama flown copper plate roof |Alternative name = Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{jalink|October 16th|Q2963}}|Shinto ritual = {{jalink|Kawazu's Tori Shojin Sake Shojin|Q11553983|Tori Shojin Sake Shojin}} ({{jalink|December 18th|Q2438}} - {{jalink|December 23rd|Q2676|23rd}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=45|lat_sec=15.3|lon_deg=138|lon_min=59|lon_sec=20.6|label=Shinto Torii
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} [[File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, otorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Otorii}}]]'''Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine'' is located in {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} in Tanaka. In {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}. '''Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine'' (not ``Kawazu'') [https://sugihokowakenomikoto.jimdo.com/ Official site of Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine (Sugisawabetsu no Mikoto Shrine)][http://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/25 Large camphor tree of Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine] - Kawazu Town Tourism Association. It is in charge of '''{{jalink|#Himemiya Shrine||Himemiya Shrine}}''', which is compared to ``Sasahara Hime no Mikoto Shrine'', a Shikinai shrine. === Company name === In ancient times, it was called ``Sawabetsu Myojin'', ``Kino (Great) Myojin'', ``Kinomiya (Great) Myojin'', ``Kino Shrine'', ``Kino Shrine'', and ``Kinosaki Myojin'', and even today it is commonly known as ``Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine''. It is said to be a shrine with {{jalink|Kinomiya faith|Q11296898}}, as it has passed down the ancient name of ``Kinomiya'' and ``Tori Shojin Sake Shojin'' (described later). === Enshrined God === The main deity is ``Sugihokowake no Mikoto'', and {{jalink|Isotake no Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Sukuhikona no Mikoto}} are enshrined in {{jalink|Aidono|Q135009883}}. In "{{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932}}", it is listed as 1 dai, so it is thought that it was originally dedicated to Sugisubetsu no Mikoto. . === History ===According to the shrine's legend, it had been enshrined since ancient times, but was rebuilt in the {{jalink|Wado|Q1373026}} period (708 - 15), and was listed as a small shrine in the Engishiki Shinmyocho. In addition, in the shrine biography, in {{jalink|Kenkyu|Q1193933}}4 ({{jalink|1193|Q19741}}), {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800}} rebuilt it and {{jalink|sharyo}}93{{jalink|stone (Unit)|Q855048|stone}} was dedicated, and the shrine building was further rebuilt by {{jalink|Fujiwara Yoritsune|Q471780}} in {{jalink|1226|Q5405}}. In {{jalink|Yasunaga|Q980266}} 2nd year ({{jalink|1343|Q6072}}), {{jalink|Domestic Shinto Name Book|Q11420490}}, ``{{jalink|Izu Province Shinto Name Book|Q17193087}},'' it is written as ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Hokowake no Myojin.'' According to the shrine's legend, the shrine was rebuilt by Yoshitane Ashikaga|Q708527} in {{jalink|Eisho|Q1193577}}10 ({{jalink|1513|Q6200}}), and 12 families served {{jalink|Shake|Q11588367}}. (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} In the 7th year ({{jalink|1538|Q6470}}), the shrine and other buildings were completely destroyed by fire, and in the 13th year of the same year ({{jalink|1544|Q652 In 5}}), after the shrine's territory was washed away by a flood, it fell into disrepair and was enshrined in a small shrine, but it is said that the governor of the country {{jalink|Yasuhide Shimizu|Q11560955}} and others rebuilt it.In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), he was appointed to Gosha. {{jalink|Postwar|Q1201472}} belongs to {{jalink|Shrine Headquarters|Q908077}}. Furthermore, the Shinto priest has been served by the {{jalink|Kawazu clan|Q11553993}} for generations. === Precincts === === Shrine building === The main shrine was rebuilt in {{jalink|Kasho|Q1373157}}2 ({{jalink|1819|Q6967}}), and is {{jalink|Nagarezukuri|Q210684}}. The roof is {{jalink|copper plate roof}} with {{jalink|gable #gable style|Q4881511|staggered gable}} and eaves {{jalink|gable #gable style|Q4881511|kara gable}}. File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, haiden.jpg|House of worship File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii in the precincts === Big Kusu of Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine === [[File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, okusu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|[[Big Kusu of Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja|Big Kusu]] (National Natural Monument)}}]] {{Main|Big Kusu of Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine}} A {{jalink|camphor tree|Q158722|camphor}} with a tree height of about 24m and a trunk circumference of 14m. The tree was estimated to be over 1,000 years old in the early {{jalink|Showa period|Q327567}} period (first half of the 20th century), and was designated as a national {{jalink|natural monument|Q23790}} in 1936|Q18649}}. Since ancient times, it has been called ``Kimiya-sama's great Kusu'' and has been worshiped as {{jalink|sacred tree|Q48743901}}. There were once seven large camphor trees in Kawazu, and until the mid-Meiji period they were called ``Nanaka Kae Nanakusu,'' but this is the only one that remains today. There is also a large camphor tree near the approach entrance and in front of the shrine to the right. In addition, there is also {{jalink|Kinomiya Shrine|Q11523682}} in {{jalink|Atami City|Q653364}} in the northern part of {{jalink|Izu Peninsula|Q822822}}. alink|Azu Sawaki Shrine's Large Kusu|Q60403026}} There is a famous camphor tree (also a natural monument), which is often called the same name as "Kimiya Shrine's Kusu", so it is easy to confuse it. File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja 20100601 (3).jpg|Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja ShrineFile:Cinnamomum camphora 20100601 (Kawazu) (B).jpg|Cinnamomum camphora at the entrance to the approach === Setsusha === Doso Shrine, Uga Shrine, Inari Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Mizu Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Kojima Shrine, Toshino Shrine, Hobutsuki Shrine, Ohgusu Shrine, Sa Shrine, Yama Shrine, Onagotsusa Shrine File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festival === Sakeru Festival (December 17th) / Sake Festival (December 24th) - Between the two festivals (December 18th - 23rd) is called {{jalink|Kawazu's Tori Shojin Sake Shojin|Q11553983}}, and it is said that when Sugisabetsu no Mikoto, who was drunk in the area, was about to burn to death in a wild fire, he was saved thanks to a large flock of small birds carrying water from their wings and extinguishing the fire, so Sugisabetsu no Mikoto forbade him to drink alcohol and eat poultry and eggs. === Himemiya Shrine === {{Shrine |Name = Himemiya Shrine |Image = [[File:Himemiya-jinja (Kawazu), shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
Shrine building
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|45|06.1|N|138|59|34.3|E}}}} |Location = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}{{jalink|Kawazu Town|Q1203861}}Sasahara 314 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|45|06.1|N|138|59|34.3|E|scale:25000|name=Himemiya Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = Sasahara Hime no Mikoto |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} |Soken = |Main shrine = Ikkensha-ryu-zukuri thatched roof |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = |Shinto ritual = }} === Company name === In ancient times, it was called ``Himemiya'' or ``Himemiya Daimyojin.'' === Enshrined God === Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine ({{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q3 13119|Osazaki no Mikoto) and Kageyama Shrine (Kageyama Myojin) were enshrined togetherProperly, it was enshrined at Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, and the main deity was changed to Sasahara Hime no Mikoto. . === History === Ritual artifacts thought to date back to the 4th century have been unearthed near the former shrine grounds (305 Sasahara Himamiya, Kawazu-machi, currently on the grounds of {{jalink|Kawazu Town Minami Elementary School}} and {{jalink|Himemiya Kindergarten}}) ({{jalink|Himemiya Ruins}}), and it appears that it has been worshiped as a {{jalink|Tsubojin|Q1392097}} since ancient times. It is listed as a small shrine in the ``Engishiki Shinmyocho'', and is described as ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper Sawarahime no Miko'' in the ``Izu Province Shinto Namecho''. According to the shrine's biography, the shrine had territory until around {{jalink|Motogame|Q1145064}} (1570 - 73), but for the {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} years (1573 - It is said that it was confiscated in 1992), and in {{jalink|Bunroku|Q1373039}}5 ({{jalink|1596|Q6785}}), a company official called Tosho took away the old records and declined. It was rebuilt by the people of the entire Kawazu village in {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 4th year ({{jalink|1664|Q7581}}), and was listed in {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} in 1896, In 1213, Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, which was located at the current site (slightly northeast of the current site), was enshrined together with the nearby Kageyama Shrine, and the company name was changed to ``Himemiya Shrine'' and the main deity was changed to ``Sasahara Hime no Mikoto''. - It was rebuilt in 2019). , In 1962, it was moved to the side of the main shrine of Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine, but it was moved again in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}14 ({{jalink|2002|Q1987}}). === Shrine building === The main shrine is one-span {{jalink|nagaru-zukuri|Q210684}} on both the front and sides, with a {{jalink|杮thatched|Q199388}} roof with a staggered gable and a kara gable on the eaves. It is a late {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} shrine building. File:Himemiya-jinja (Kawazu), kyushati.jpg|Kawazu Town Minami Elementary School (Himemiya Ruins) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === References === *Shikinaisha Research Report, edited by Shikinaisha Research Association, Volume 10, Izu Province, Kai Province, Kogakkan University Publishing Department, 1981. *Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, edited by Kenichi Tanigawa, Volume 10, Tokai, Hakusuisha, 1987 ISBN 4-560-02220-8 === Transportation === *Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine is a 15-minute walk from {{jalink|Izukyu Line|Q864265}}{{jalink|Kawazu Station|Q6379913}}. Along the way is the entrance to Himemiya Shrine. === External link === {{Commonscat|Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja}} {{Commonscat|Himemiya-jinja (Kawazu)|Himemiya Shrine}} *[https://sugihokowakenomikoto.jimdoweb.com/ Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine (Sugisawa Betsu no Mikoto Shrine)] - Official website * {{Twitter|kawazukinomiya|Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine}} * {{Instagram|kawazukinomiyashrine|Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine (Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine)}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Redirect belongs to category |redirect1=Himemiya Shrine (Kawazu Town) |1-1=Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture |1-2=Shikinaisha in Izunokuni |1-3=Old village shrine in Shizuoka prefecture |1-4=Buildings in Kawazu Town |1-5=History of Kawazu Town }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sukihokowa Mikoto Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:河津町の建築物]] [[Category:河津町の歴史]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

'''Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine'' (not ``Kawazu'') [https://sugihokowakenomikoto.jimdo.com/ Official site of Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine (Sugisawabetsu no Mikoto Shrine)][http://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/25 Large camphor tree of Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine] - Kawazu Town Tourism Association. It is in charge of '''{{ill|lt=#Himemiya Shrine||WD=|lt=Himemiya Shrine}}''', which is compared to ``Sasahara Hime no Mikoto Shrine'', a Shikinai shrine. === Company name === In ancient times, it was called ``Sawabetsu Myojin'', ``Kino (Great) Myojin'', ``Kinomiya (Great) Myojin'', ``Kino Shrine'', ``Kino Shrine'', and ``Kinosaki Myojin'', and even today it is commonly known as ``Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine''. It is said to be a shrine with {{ill|lt=Kinomiya faith|Q11296898|WD=Q11296898}}, as it has passed down the ancient name of ``Kinomiya'' and ``Tori Shojin Sake Shojin'' (described later). === Enshrined God === The main deity is ``Sugihokowake no Mikoto'', and {{ill||lt=Isotake no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} and {{ill||lt=Sukuhikona no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} are enshrined in {{ill|lt=Aidono|Q135009883|WD=Q135009883}}. In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}", it is listed as 1 dai, so it is thought that it was originally dedicated to Sugisubetsu no Mikoto. . === History ===According to the shrine's legend, it had been enshrined since ancient times, but was rebuilt in the {{ill|lt=Wado|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026}} period (708 - 15), and was listed as a small shrine in the Engishiki Shinmyocho. In addition, in the shrine biography, in {{ill|lt=Kenkyu|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933}}4 ({{ill|lt=1193|Q19741|WD=Q19741}}), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800}} rebuilt it and {{ill||lt=sharyo|UNKNOWN}}93{{ill|lt=stone (Unit)|Q855048|WD=Q855048|lt=stone}} was dedicated, and the shrine building was further rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara Yoritsune|Q471780|WD=Q471780}} in {{ill|lt=1226|Q5405|WD=Q5405}}. In {{ill|lt=Yasunaga|Q980266|WD=Q980266}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1343|Q6072|WD=Q6072}}), {{ill|lt=Domestic Shinto Name Book|Q11420490|WD=Q11420490}}, ``{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shinto Name Book|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}},'' it is written as ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Hokowake no Myojin.'' According to the shrine's legend, the shrine was rebuilt by Yoshitane Ashikaga|Q708527} in {{ill|lt=Eisho|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577}}10 ({{ill|lt=1513|Q6200|WD=Q6200}}), and 12 families served {{ill|lt=Shake|Q11588367|WD=Q11588367}}. (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} In the 7th year ({{ill|lt=1538|Q6470|WD=Q6470}}), the shrine and other buildings were completely destroyed by fire, and in the 13th year of the same year ({{ill||lt=1544|UNKNOWN}}), after the shrine's territory was washed away by a flood, it fell into disrepair and was enshrined in a small shrine, but it is said that the governor of the country {{ill|lt=Yasuhide Shimizu|Q11560955|WD=Q11560955}} and others rebuilt it.In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), he was appointed to Gosha. {{ill|lt=Postwar|Q1201472|WD=Q1201472}} belongs to {{ill|lt=Shrine Headquarters|Q908077|WD=Q908077}}. Furthermore, the Shinto priest has been served by the {{ill|lt=Kawazu clan|Q11553993|WD=Q11553993}} for generations. === Precincts === === Shrine building === The main shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Kasho|Q1373157|WD=Q1373157}}2 ({{ill|lt=1819|Q6967|WD=Q6967}}), and is {{ill|lt=Nagarezukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}. The roof is {{ill||lt=copper plate roof|UNKNOWN}} with {{ill|lt=gable #gable style|Q4881511|WD=Q4881511|lt=staggered gable}} and eaves {{ill|lt=gable #gable style|Q4881511|WD=Q4881511|lt=kara gable}}. File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, haiden.jpg|House of worship File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii in the precincts === Big Kusu of Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine === [[File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, okusu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|[[Big Kusu of Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja|Big Kusu]] (National Natural Monument)}}]] {{Main|Big Kusu of Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine}} A {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|lt=camphor}} with a tree height of about 24m and a trunk circumference of 14m. The tree was estimated to be over 1,000 years old in the early {{ill|lt=Showa period|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} period (first half of the 20th century), and was designated as a national {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}} in 1936|Q18649}}. Since ancient times, it has been called ``Kimiya-sama's great Kusu'' and has been worshiped as {{ill|lt=sacred tree|Q48743901|WD=Q48743901}}. There were once seven large camphor trees in Kawazu, and until the mid-Meiji period they were called ``Nanaka Kae Nanakusu,'' but this is the only one that remains today. There is also a large camphor tree near the approach entrance and in front of the shrine to the right. In addition, there is also {{ill|lt=Kinomiya Shrine|Q11523682|WD=Q11523682}} in {{ill|lt=Atami City|Q653364|WD=Q653364}} in the northern part of {{ill|lt=Izu Peninsula|Q822822|WD=Q822822}}. alink|Azu Sawaki Shrine's Large Kusu|Q60403026}} There is a famous camphor tree (also a natural monument), which is often called the same name as "Kimiya Shrine's Kusu", so it is easy to confuse it. File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja 20100601 (3).jpg|Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja ShrineFile:Cinnamomum camphora 20100601 (Kawazu) (B).jpg|Cinnamomum camphora at the entrance to the approach === Setsusha === Doso Shrine, Uga Shrine, Inari Shrine, Tenjin Shrine, Mizu Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Kojima Shrine, Toshino Shrine, Hobutsuki Shrine, Ohgusu Shrine, Sa Shrine, Yama Shrine, Onagotsusa Shrine File:Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festival === Sakeru Festival (December 17th) / Sake Festival (December 24th) - Between the two festivals (December 18th - 23rd) is called {{ill|lt=Kawazu's Tori Shojin Sake Shojin|Q11553983|WD=Q11553983}}, and it is said that when Sugisabetsu no Mikoto, who was drunk in the area, was about to burn to death in a wild fire, he was saved thanks to a large flock of small birds carrying water from their wings and extinguishing the fire, so Sugisabetsu no Mikoto forbade him to drink alcohol and eat poultry and eggs. === Himemiya Shrine === {{Shrine |Name = Himemiya Shrine |Image = [[File:Himemiya-jinja (Kawazu), shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
Shrine building
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|45|06.1|N|138|59|34.3|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}{{ill|lt=Kawazu Town|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861}}Sasahara 314 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|45|06.1|N|138|59|34.3|E|scale:25000|name=Himemiya Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = Sasahara Hime no Mikoto |Company rank = {{ill||lt=Shikiuchisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
Old {{ill|lt=murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Soken = |Main shrine = Ikkensha-ryu-zukuri thatched roof |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = |Shinto ritual = }} === Company name === In ancient times, it was called ``Himemiya'' or ``Himemiya Daimyojin.'' === Enshrined God === Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine ({{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q3 13119|Osazaki no Mikoto) and Kageyama Shrine (Kageyama Myojin) were enshrined togetherProperly, it was enshrined at Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, and the main deity was changed to Sasahara Hime no Mikoto. . === History === Ritual artifacts thought to date back to the 4th century have been unearthed near the former shrine grounds (305 Sasahara Himamiya, Kawazu-machi, currently on the grounds of {{ill||lt=Kawazu Town Minami Elementary School|UNKNOWN}} and {{ill||lt=Himemiya Kindergarten|UNKNOWN}}) ({{ill||lt=Himemiya Ruins|UNKNOWN}}), and it appears that it has been worshiped as a {{ill|lt=Tsubojin|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097}} since ancient times. It is listed as a small shrine in the ``Engishiki Shinmyocho'', and is described as ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper Sawarahime no Miko'' in the ``Izu Province Shinto Namecho''. According to the shrine's biography, the shrine had territory until around {{ill|lt=Motogame|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064}} (1570 - 73), but for the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} years (1573 - It is said that it was confiscated in 1992), and in {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039}}5 ({{ill|lt=1596|Q6785|WD=Q6785}}), a company official called Tosho took away the old records and declined. It was rebuilt by the people of the entire Kawazu village in {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 4th year ({{ill|lt=1664|Q7581|WD=Q7581}}), and was listed in {{ill|lt=murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} in 1896, In 1213, Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine, which was located at the current site (slightly northeast of the current site), was enshrined together with the nearby Kageyama Shrine, and the company name was changed to ``Himemiya Shrine'' and the main deity was changed to ``Sasahara Hime no Mikoto''. - It was rebuilt in 2019). , In 1962, it was moved to the side of the main shrine of Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine, but it was moved again in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}}14 ({{ill|lt=2002|Q1987|WD=Q1987}}). === Shrine building === The main shrine is one-span {{ill|lt=nagaru-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} on both the front and sides, with a {{ill|lt=杮thatched|Q199388|WD=Q199388}} roof with a staggered gable and a kara gable on the eaves. It is a late {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} shrine building. File:Himemiya-jinja (Kawazu), kyushati.jpg|Kawazu Town Minami Elementary School (Himemiya Ruins) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === References === *Shikinaisha Research Report, edited by Shikinaisha Research Association, Volume 10, Izu Province, Kai Province, Kogakkan University Publishing Department, 1981. *Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, edited by Kenichi Tanigawa, Volume 10, Tokai, Hakusuisha, 1987 ISBN 4-560-02220-8 === Transportation === *Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Izukyu Line|Q864265|WD=Q864265}}{{ill|lt=Kawazu Station|Q6379913|WD=Q6379913}}. Along the way is the entrance to Himemiya Shrine. === External link === {{Commonscat|Sugihokowakenomikoto-jinja}} {{Commonscat|Himemiya-jinja (Kawazu)|Himemiya Shrine}} *[https://sugihokowakenomikoto.jimdoweb.com/ Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine (Sugisawa Betsu no Mikoto Shrine)] - Official website * {{Twitter|kawazukinomiya|Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine}} * {{Instagram|kawazukinomiyashrine|Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine (Sugisubetsu no Mikoto Shrine)}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Redirect belongs to category |redirect1=Himemiya Shrine (Kawazu Town) |1-1=Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture |1-2=Shikinaisha in Izunokuni |1-3=Old village shrine in Shizuoka prefecture |1-4=Buildings in Kawazu Town |1-5=History of Kawazu Town }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sukihokowa Mikoto Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:河津町の建築物]] [[Category:河津町の歴史]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]
